# SignalChord v1.0.0

SignalChord v1.0.0 is the first public-alpha source release of the repository-owned article-to-alert reference path.

## Included

- permitted RSS/Atom discovery, document fetching and Kafka normalization;
- immutable source storage, deduplication and versioned Protobuf contracts;
- deterministic extraction, entity resolution and claim processing;
- idempotent Neo4j and OpenSearch projection with query and analytics APIs;
- Rails identity, tenancy, RBAC, sources, watchlists, policies, alerts and audit APIs;
- constrained Velato policy execution, five functional musical policies, executable MIDI downloads and the Policy Studio playback easter egg;
- React web, Expo mobile and authenticated realtime clients;
- Docker Compose integration, Kubernetes/Helm deployment material and a single-owner k3s profile;
- source and image SBOMs, high/critical vulnerability scans, immutable image digests, keyless Cosign signatures, signed SLSA provenance, checksums and a release manifest.

## Supported use

This release is suitable for source evaluation, local development, controlled staging and community self-hosting trials. The verified reference path is deterministic and uses repository-owned fixtures and contracts.

## Important limitations

This release is not certification for an internet-facing, highly available production service or for processing real customer data. Operators remain responsible for source rights, unique secrets, trusted TLS, data-protection controls, retention, capacity validation, monitoring, incident response and tested backup/restore in their own target environment.

The included single-server profile is intentionally single-owner and single-node. It is not a multi-operator or highly available security boundary.

## Install

For local evaluation:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
./scripts/dev-up.sh
./scripts/smoke-test.sh
```

For the single-server path, follow `docs/single-server-kubernetes.md` and use only the immutable image digests published with this release.

## Upgrade and rollback

This is the first stable source tag, so there is no earlier stable schema upgrade path. Before adopting a later release, create and verify a backup using the versioned single-server backup tooling. Roll back application images by immutable digest and restore data only with the documented version-compatible restore procedure.

## Security

Do not reuse Compose development credentials outside local development. Report vulnerabilities privately according to `SECURITY.md`; do not disclose suspected vulnerabilities in public issues.
